pages.title: docs.snyk.io
pages.content: Homepage | Snyk User Docs ⌘ Ctrl k Support Snyk Learn API reference Product updates Sign up for free More Discover Snyk Platform administration Scan, fix, and prevent Developer tools Agent security Snyk data and governance Homepage What's Snyk? Glossary Snyk release process What's new? Snyk 2.0 platform improvements Supported languages Supported languages, package managers, and frameworks Technical specifications and guidance Supported languages list Getting started guides Getting started Structure your account for high application performance Start scanning Getting started with the REST API Implementation guides Overview Auto-provisioning guide Team implementation guide Enterprise implementation guide Developer education with Snyk Learn Developer security education and Snyk product training with Snyk Learn Powered by GitBook On this page For the complete documentation index, see llms.txt . This page is also available as Markdown . Copy On this page Homepage Scan, prioritize, and fix vulnerabilities in your code, open-source dependencies, container images, and cloud configurations. Check out the latest updates in the Snyk user documentation . Discover Snyk New to Snyk? Learn what it is, what's new, what languages are supported, and how to get started. Platform administration Set up and run Snyk: SSO, Snyk hierarchy, user roles, and Snyk Broker. Scan, fix, and prevent Find, fix, and prevent issues across your software development lifecycle. Prioritize fixes and enforce policies. Developer tools Bring security into your workflow with the Snyk CLI, IDE plugins, SCM and CI/CD integrations, and the Snyk API. Agent security Secure AI-driven development with Snyk Studio, Agent Scan, and Agent Guard. Snyk data and governance How Snyk handles your data: generative AI use, residency, and compliance. Next What's Snyk? Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? More information Snyk privacy policy © 2026 Snyk Limited | All product and company names and logos are trademarks of their respective owners. Was this helpful?
